body: Peter, I'm sick and tired of the same old liberal rhetoric coming out of this week's Democratic convention. Unfortunately, we're only going to hear more and more of these liberal lies between now and Election Day. We'll hear that we're wrong about everything. But I have to ask: what's right about the liberal policies of high unemployment rates, sluggish economic growth, and growing insecurity around the world? The American people are sick and tired of the Obama administration - that's why we must win back the White House. And when Donald Trump wins the White House, we must ensure he has a conservative majority in Congress to pass real reform. But if we don't step up RIGHT NOW - that future looks to be very much in doubt. That's why, effective immediately, I've authorized DOUBLE-MATCH status for all contributions until midnight on Sunday. name: Ben Carson
subject: This was a direct attack on Christianity:
body:
Peter,

I'm sure you've heard of the horrific news out of France, where
an 84-year-old priest was viciously murdered and four others were
taken hostage by two "soldiers" of ISIS.

In the eyes of the Islamic radicals, what were they guilty of?
Nothing but being Christians.

This was a direct, unapologetic attack on Christianity and it
should serve as a reminder to every Christian here in America
that in many ways, we have it easy.

In many places across the globe, Christians are being persecuted
and even killed for what they believe. Fortunately, that is not
the case here in America.

We have the freedom to live by our values, and if we feel that
our leaders aren't respecting them, all we need to do is vote. To
think that millions of Christians have not taken full advantage
of their suffrage has been a huge disappointment to me, and it is
a big reason that I decided to serve as the Honorary National
Chairman of My Faith Votes.

America is an exceptional nation not because we are better than
anyone else. We are, in fact, an exception to all of human
history in that our founding document recognizes that we are
endowed by our Creator with certain unalienable rights.

Paramount among them is our right to live by our faith without
fear of government persecution. It is my goal that through My
Faith Votes, we can inspire millions of believers to take full
advantage of this freedom, because as we have seen around the
world, it is not and will never be guaranteed.

Thank you for your time, and I hope we can count on your
continued support.

It is your commitment and generosity that allows us to continue
our mission, and I'm honored to have you on our side.

Sincerely,

Ben Carson

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Ready, Peter?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Accepting the nomination | Peter, last night it was my honor to humbly accept my party’s nomination to be the next Vice President of the United States.

I’m ready to work for America to be a community of respect, where people are loved and not demeaned because of their race, religion, national origin, disability, gender, or sexual orientation.

I’m ready to work for the bright future our country can have if only we build it together.

And I’m ready to work to elect the leader who can take us there: Hillary Clinton.

[**If you’re ready to work for all that, too, I hope you’ll chip in right now -- and when you do, we’ll send you a free sticker to say thanks.**

](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=14&ms=MjYwOTM1ODkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQ0MTUwMDE3S0&mt=1&rt=0) The weight of this moment sits a little heavier on my shoulders because my son, Nat, can’t be with me to share it -- his Marine battalion deployed three days ago to protect and defend the very NATO allies that Donald Trump has said he might abandon.

Last night, when I accepted this nomination, I thought of Nat and all the members of our armed forces who are counting on our commander in chief to make responsible decisions to keep them safe.

I thought about my parents, my in-laws, and every senior citizen who hopes for a dignified retirement with strong health care and research to end Alzheimer’s and other diseases.

I thought about my children, Woody and Annella, and all young people hoping for a strong future where they can find good jobs without the crushing burden of student debt.

And I thought about my brilliant wife, Anne, and every strong woman who has fought to make her voice heard, to make her own decisions, and to make history in this election.

I know Hillary is ready to fight for all of us. I use the word “ready” for a specific reason: When I lived in Honduras, I learned that the best compliment you could give someone was to say they were “listo” -- ready. Because what “listo” means in Spanish is prepared, battle-tested, rock-solid, up for anything, never backing down. And Hillary Clinton is “lista.”

She’s ready because of her faith, her heart, and her experience. She’s ready because she knows America can be more prosperous together, safer together, stronger together.

**Hillary is ready. And so are we. Today, let’s show her we’re with her.** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MjYwOTM1ODkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQ0MTUwMDE3S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you, Peter. I can’t wait to work with you to build the bright future that all our children deserve.

Tim

|

clean_body: Accepting the nomination | Peter, last night it was my honor to humbly accept my party’s nomination to be the next Vice President of the United States. I’m ready to work for America to be a community of respect, where people are loved and not demeaned because of their race, religion, national origin, disability, gender, or sexual orientation. I’m ready to work for the bright future our country can have if only we build it together. And I’m ready to work to elect the leader who can take us there: Hillary Clinton. The weight of this moment sits a little heavier on my shoulders because my son, Nat, can’t be with me to share it -- his Marine battalion deployed three days ago to protect and defend the very NATO allies that Donald Trump has said he might abandon. Last night, when I accepted this nomination, I thought of Nat and all the members of our armed forces who are counting on our commander in chief to make responsible decisions to keep them safe. I thought about my parents, my in-laws, and every senior citizen who hopes for a dignified retirement with strong health care and research to end Alzheimer’s and other diseases. I thought about my children, Woody and Annella, and all young people hoping for a strong future where they can find good jobs without the crushing burden of student debt. And I thought about my brilliant wife, Anne, and every strong woman who has fought to make her voice heard, to make her own decisions, and to make history in this election. I know Hillary is ready to fight for all of us. I use the word “ready” for a specific reason: When I lived in Honduras, I learned that the best compliment you could give someone was to say they were “listo” -- ready. Because what “listo” means in Spanish is prepared, battle-tested, rock-solid, up for anything, never backing down. And Hillary Clinton is “lista.” She’s ready because of her faith, her heart, and her experience. She’s ready because she knows America can be more prosperous together, safer together, stronger together. Hillary is ready. And so are we. Today, let’s show her we’re with her. Thank you, Peter. I can’t wait to work with you to build the bright future that all our children deserve. body:
This election is not going to be easy. | Peter --

I hope you're loving our convention -- certainly a big step up from the doom and gloom of last week. But there is something else you need to see:

[](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjYwOTg3MjQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQ0MjM4NzEwS0&mt=1&rt=0)

As always, we take individual surveys with a big grain of salt -- but an average across this many polls tells us something important. There's a trend in this race -- and it shows that Donald Trump is in a very strong position coming out of his convention.

So as you watch the last night of our convention, remember this election is not going to be easy. If you look at Trump and just can't imagine him winning -- that's the kind of thinking that could lose this race for us. Keep this in mind every single day: It's going to be close, it’s going to be hard, and we’re going to need everyone to step up -- otherwise we’re going to be seeing a lot more red polls.

Are you watching the Democratic National Convention? [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTI2NzUxNwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTYyMjQ2OTUwS0&mt=1&rt=0)
| Friend, I hope you're watching some of what's happening in Philadelphia this week. From Elizabeth Warren and Bernie Sanders, to Bill Clinton and Michelle Obama, we Democrats have spent the last few days offering a bold, progressive vision for our country. And make no mistake, we've got a lot of work ahead of us -- fighting for working people, improving our schools, working toward environmental, racial, and gender justice. It's a bold vision _and_ it's within our grasp. **But for that vision to become a reality across the country, it must start with progress in our states -- the laboratories of democracy, as Louis Brandeis once wrote.** It's why I'm so proud of what we've accomplished these last four years in Washington state, like leading the clean energy economy and securing all-day kindergarten for Washington kids. And it's why I'm fighting so hard this election -- because we have so much more left to do. If you're proud, like I am, of the Democratic agenda, of the progress we've won in Washington, and of the future we are writing together, I need to ask you something right now. We're still 221 donations away from our big end-of-month deadline -- the last official deadline before the primary. [**Can I count on you to help put us over the top?**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=OTI2NzUxNwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTYyMjQ2OTUwS0&mt=1&rt=0) _If you 've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [EXPRESS DONATE $10 »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=OTI2NzUxNwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTYyMjQ2OTUwS0&mt=2&rj=OTYyMjQ2OTUwS0&rt=0)

name: Mike Weinholtz
subject: Mike Weinholtz for Governor Newsletter + New Video!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mike Weinholtz for Governor
body:
| | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/5820177942076132096/5072689246977067776)
---
MIKE WEINHOLTZ FOR GOVERNOR NEWSLETTER Haz click [aqui](https://act.myngp.com/el/5820177942076132096/5144746841014995712) para leerlo en español! MESSAGE FROM MIKE Can you believe that summer is almost over? Time flies on the campaign trail, but I'm very proud to report that my campaign staff is running at full steam, and exceeding expectations left and right. I'm in Philadelphia this week for the Democratic National Convention. I'm here to meet with the Democratic Governors Association. I've also been traveling all around Utah, meeting with voters to listen to what matters to them - their values, their needs, and their frustration with the way our current governor and legislature have been neglecting them. I'm a firm believer that every person has a story to tell, and that there's a lot to be learned by listening honestly to those stories. I'm very proud to announce the release of our newest campaign video, which is part of my own story - where I come from, and why I'm running for governor. Check it out here: [](https://act.myngp.com/el/5820177942076132096/5216804435052923648?v=ydPaJ0hPWqk) This is the year we decide how great our future will be in Utah! Mike P.S. If you like our new video, be sure to share it on your own social media so we can reach enough Utah voters to take back our state in November!
* * *
CHECK OUT OUR NEW PODCAST! Who doesn't love podcasts, right? Part of my strategy for winning this election is making sure we use not only traditional media outlets (like television, radio, and print) but new methods as well-- like YouTube, Instagram, Facebook Live, and most recently: our very own podcast! Tune in to our weekly conversation with Mike and Kim (available on iTunes [HERE](https://act.myngp.com/el/5820177942076132096/5288862029090851584) or via GooglePlay [HERE](https://act.myngp.com/el/5820177942076132096/5360919623128779520?t=The_Candidates_Podcast__Mike_for_Utah).)
* * *
UPDATES FROM THE ROAD Kim and I have spent a lot of time on the road recently! Almost 1 million Utahns live outside of the Wasatch Front, and while we both have plenty of demands on our schedules without extra traveling, we want to hear from ALL Utahns! I was able to travel to Bluff, Utah, two weeks back for a meeting with U.S. Secretary of the Interior Sally Jewell, about the controversial Bears Ears monument. Gov. Herbert wasn't there, but plenty of Utahns - from both sides of the debate - were there to ensure Secretary Jewell heard their voices.
Meanwhile, Kim has been traveling far and wide to meet and listen to voters! Here are some pics of him in Hyrum, Beaver, Provo, and more: Click [HERE](https://act.myngp.com/el/5820177942076132096/5432977217166707456) to see upcoming campaign events!
* * *

GRASSROOTS VOTER OUTREACH! In case you haven't already heard, our campaign is launching an unprecedented grassroots voter outreach effort! No campaign in Utah has ever knocked on more than 150,000 doors, but my team has set its sights high and is going to knock on 500,000 doors. Let me repeat that: my campaign for Governor is going to knock on 500,000 doors in Utah. To put that in perspective, that represents well over 1 in every 3 Utahns expected to vote this November. We're using cutting-edge tech solutions and grassroots organizing strategies. We have over 20 canvassers and anticipate doubling that number before the election. I also want to add that all of our canvassers are equipped to register voters. Utah has one of the lowest voter turnouts in Utah, and we're committed to giving people both a reason to vote as well as a little help getting registered if they need it! You can help! If you're interested in joining the team, we're still accepting applications [HERE](https://act.myngp.com/el/5820177942076132096/5505034811204635392?c=0&w=1)\-- or you can [sign up to volunteer](https://act.myngp.com/el/5820177942076132096/5577092405242563328) whenever your schedule allows.
* * *

MIKE FOR UTAH DIVERSITY TEAM In June, the campaign launched our Diversity Team! These are the folks who are working overtime to help us ensure that we end the exclusion of minority voices from our Utah democracy! It's very important to me personally that my campaign makes opportunities to listen to ALL Utahns and represent their concerns, so I'm thrilled about this brilliant new team. Check out this Facebook Live discussion with team members Ernie, Margarita, Angie, and Alejandro to hear about the awesome work they're doing: [](https://act.myngp.com/el/5820177942076132096/5649149999280491264?type=2&theater)
Be sure to visit and "Like" our pages: [Latinos for Mike](https://act.myngp.com/el/5820177942076132096/5721207593318419200), [Pacific Islanders for Mike](https://act.myngp.com/el/5820177942076132096/5793265187356347136), [Asian Americans for Mike](https://act.myngp.com/el/5820177942076132096/5865322781394275072), and stay tuned for an "African-Americans for Mike" page coming soon!
* * *

My campaign isn't taking any corporate money, which means that the only way we can win is through support from individuals like you! Click [here](https://act.myngp.com/el/5820177942076132096/5937380375432203008) to contribute now!

subject: Are you excited? Make a call!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
|
Team --

Our Call Team has been a huge part of helping us get the word out about the campaign. It's one of the ways we've built an organization that can win in November -- thank you so much for being part of it.

| **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
JULY 28, 2016**

**Media Contact:**
Ethan Latour
[ethan@philscott.org](mailto:ethan@philscott.org)
802-595-3776
**Phil Scott Highlights Commitment to Road and Bridge Infrastructure, Opposition to Higher Transportation Taxes & Carbon Tax **

**_Vehicle and Automotive Distributors Association Endorses Phil Scott for Governor_**
So. Burlington, Vt.– Phil Scott today laid out his plan for improving Vermont’s roads and bridges, and reiterated his opposition to higher transportation taxes, a carbon tax and infrastructure debt supported by his opponents.

At an event where he also accepted the endorsement of the Vermont Vehicle and Automotive Distributors Association, Scott highlighted his work to reduce the Legislature’s raid on the Transportation Fund and outlined his plan for transportation when he serves as Governor.

“I am proud to have VADA’s endorsement. Supporting small businesses, and making Vermont more affordable for families and business, is critical to growing our economy, so I applaud VADA’s ongoing work to champion transportation related businesses, and fighting to ensure those businesses and their employees thrive,” Scott said. “Transportation is a key piece of growing the economy and making Vermont more affordable and I appreciate the opportunity to release my priorities in this area.”

Highlights of Scott’s plan include:

* Strengthening the link between economic growth and infrastructure with a comprehensive plan that streamlines processes and prioritizes maintenance to reduce costs;
* Opposing new or higher transportation taxes;
* Ensuring the transportation fund is not raided and is managed properly; and
* Encouraging innovation in transportation and other sectors by restoring the full research and development tax credit.

To read Phil Scott's transportation plan click [here](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=36dfa80f26&e=19cebfe173).

“Our decision to support Phil, and support Phil before the primary, was about sending a signal to Vermonters about which candidate has the ability and experience to strengthen the economy, make Vermont more affordable and run a campaign that is an example for our children,” said Marilyn Miller, executive director of the association. “Our members can see through the DC-style attack ads and know Phil does not, and will not, support any new taxes or policies that will add to Vermont’s crisis of affordability. We’ve known Phil for many years. He always does what he says he will do. And he is the person Vermont needs in the Governor’s office to restore balance in Montpelier and faith and trust in government.”

**Listen, Learn, Lead: Meet Phil Scott**
Lieutenant Governor Phil Scott has been a consistent voice of reason and common sense in Montpelier. As the only Republican elected to statewide office, Phil has shown determination and leadership in advocating for Vermonters and promoting policies and programs that benefit Vermont families and the economy. Read about Phil Scott’s plans at [www.philscott.org](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=7e5e4ffd6d&e=19cebfe173) **About the Vermont Vehicle and Automotive Distributors Association** The Vermont Vehicle and Automotive Distributors Association (VADA) is a trade association representing the interests of the automotive industry including new car and truck dealers, along with motorcycle, recreational vehicle, farm equipment, used-car, power equipment, and construction equipment dealers, as well as motor vehicle service, auto body repair, and motor vehicle parts sales facilities.

Team —

I just stepped off the main stage at the Democratic Convention, where I stood face to face with Democrats from around the country — and let me tell you, I am fired up and ready to win this race.

This campaign started small, right here in Philadelphia, a few people in a cramped office determined to fight for hardworking families and share our message of fairness, equality, and progress.

Tonight, because of our thousands of grassroots supporters — just like you — I was able to bring our message to the nation.

subject: We need you, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Progress is not a solo activity | Peter --

More than 50 years ago, I left to march in Selma knowing that I could come back home with scars -- or not at all. When I was hit in the head with a night stick by a state trooper at the foot of the Edmund Pettus Bridge, I thought I was going to die.

But I went because I knew that it was the right and important thing to do. That silence in the face of injustice is another form of injustice.

This is something that Hillary Clinton has understood her entire life. It’s what drove her to go to South Carolina and fight against the harmful practice of sending minors to adult prisons. It’s why she went to Beijing and declared that “women’s rights are human rights” in the face of great opposition.

It’s why I’m proud to call her my friend, and why I was so honored to stand on stage at the Democratic National Convention and nominate her as our next president. She’s taken up the mantle so many times over that I’ve lost count, and she’ll continue that fight from the White House.

**But progress is not a solo activity, Peter. It’s a team sport, and we’re counting on you right now. body:
Democrats Attack Greitens Days Before Missouri GOP Primary

By Maggie Severns, POLITICO
7/28/2016 5:17 PM EST

A political group affiliated with the Democratic Governors Association is reserving ad time to attack GOP gubernatorial candidate Eric Greitens in Missouri, seeking to affect the race just five days before Republicans choose their nominee from a crowded field.

Two sources tracking media spending in the state said that Jobs and Opportunity, a 527 group funded by the DGA, has booked about a half-million dollars in broadcast TV and additional radio spending attacking Greitens. The DGA did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

The ad is set to start airing Friday. Audio, which a source said was from the ad, criticizes Greitens for accepting money from a donor accused of sexually abusing a woman.

"What does this victim accused of human trafficking have to do with your vote for governor? Well a man accused of abusing her for 13 years gave Eric Greitens $1 million," a narrator says in the ad. "While others returned his money, Greitens said he was honored to take it."

Greitens, a former Navy SEAL and political newcomer, has been picking up steam in the competitive GOP primary because of his charisma and status as an outsider. The ad is a clear indication that Democrats would rather not face Greitens in the general election. Polls have found the race between Greitens and the three other candidates extremely close, but a SurveyUSA poll released this week did find Greitens leading the pack with 25 percent, with opponent John Brunner behind him at 21 percent.

Missouri was the site of a notorious instance of Democratic primary meddling four years ago, when Democratic Sen. Claire McCaskill's campaign aired ads casting GOP Rep. Todd Akin as conservative before his Senate primary. Akin won the three-way Republican race and promptly imploded in the general election, losing to McCaskill by more than 15 percentage points.

The DGA tried to influence Republicans' gubernatorial primary in Colorado in 2014, funding a group that tried to boost conservative ex-Rep. Tom Tancredo. But the effort prompted a backlash and Tancredo ultimately lost the GOP primary.

The winner in the Missouri GOP primary will face current Attorney General Chris Koster, a moderate and rising star in the Democratic Party, in the general election.

subject: I’m so glad you’re here, Peter

body:
This is the real world. | Peter --

I make television filled with the kinds of characters I imagine we all can be. Strong, but flawed. Human, but extraordinary.

Those characters are on television. But the real world? The real world has Hillary Clinton.

Hillary is a bona fide, rolls-up-her-sleeves, fights-for-what’s-right, in-it-for-you, won’t-back-down champion who’s spent her entire career working to make this country better for all of us.

She just became the first woman in history to win a major party’s presidential nomination. This November, if we work hard enough, she’s going to win this election.
